title: MCP Architecture, Negotiation, and Trust Boundaries · Protocol Recall
summary: Trace one tool call across host, client, server, and external system. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for an MCP architecture and threat-boundary diagram after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish host, per-server client, server, model, user, and external service responsibilities.
Trace initialization, capability negotiation, requests, responses, notifications, and errors.
Advertise and negotiate only implemented protocol capabilities.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Local MCP protocol and tool-contract portfolio."""

# Add typed, bounded protocol increments below.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Host Client Server evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Host Client Server assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Distinguish host, per-server client, server, model, user, and external service responsibilities. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: agent ext mcp architecture. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Host Client Server, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to an MCP architecture and threat-boundary diagram.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: The MCP server is the model host. This fails the Host Client Server contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

stimulus.title: Spec Versioning case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: agent ext mcp architecture. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: The newest announced release candidate is already the stable protocol.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Target a named stable specification and isolate release-candidate changes. Artifact under review: an MCP architecture and threat-boundary diagram.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Spec Versioning, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Spec Versioning applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Target a named stable specification and isolate release-candidate changes.
